$description: Design tokens for Colin McCormick's signature presentation frame. Measured from BUS3000 (fully decoded); font system corroborated by the ASML MGMT-3000 deck. NOT derived from template.key, which is a different, Helvetica-based template.
$units: px in native 1920x1080 point-space (Keynote treats 1 slide point = 1 px at 1920x1080). 'frac' values are fractions of canvas for resolution-independent layout.

grid.note: Near-uniform ~90-95px frame on all four sides. Content column is 1730px centered.

kicker_top.role: section/metadata label band, hugs top margin

footer_bot.role: byline (left) + page number (right), hugs bottom margin

families.$standard: Colin's canonical three-voice set. The font NAMES are the standard recorded here; the actual font files live in the mosaics repo and are NOT merged into this token file.

sans.names: Neue Haas Grotesk (Display or Text), or Helvetica Neue LT
sans.css_stack: "Neue Haas Grotesk Display Pro", "Neue Haas Display", "Neue Haas Grotesk Text Pro", "Helvetica Neue LT Com", "Helvetica Neue", system-ui, sans-serif
sans.license: LICENSED (commercial). Installed on Colin's machine; not a system default.

serif.role: editorial / supporting voice: takeaways, captions, supporting copy

mono.css_stack: "TT Commons Pro Mono", ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace
mono.license: LICENSED (TypeType). SF Mono is the system fallback.
mono.role: ONLY metadata: kicker/eyebrow labels, class, name, section, page number

families.$history: The older Keynote decks (BUS3000 Pres.key, ASML, INFO-3110) used SF Mono for mono and NeueHaasDisplay for sans, with no serif. The three-voice set above is the current canonical standard.

statement.role: the big sentence on a slide

furniture.description: Persistent chrome set once on the master and inherited by every slide. This is the consistent identity across decks.

byline.example: BY DYLAN AKAU, REV HILL & COLIN MCCORMICK

split_panel.use: left graphic zone + right text zone (the 'bento'-adjacent move)

layouts.$source: Measured from the BUS 3000 PowerPoints (McCormick_Presentation_C-1.pptx, v4 FINAL Friends of Chamber Music) — slide 13.33 x 7.5 in (16:9). Front page is a full-bleed designed vector graphic (Adobe Illustrator EMF); the bottom bar is PowerPoint master chrome.

title_page.$note: The front page. Same every single time. Shipped as ONE full-bleed designed graphic (Illustrator art placed at off 0,0, full slide), so the four corner labels are vector art inside the graphic, not live text.
title_page.if_rebuilt_live: mono labels pinned to the four corners on the standard side/top margin; DU logo as an image top-right

footer_bar.$note: The gray bar at the bottom. ALWAYS bottom, never a header at the top. PowerPoint master-level chrome, so it repeats on every content slide automatically.
footer_bar.position: full-width band pinned to the bottom edge
footer_bar.rule: page number + section live in this bottom bar — never set as text at the top of the slide

bento.$note: Library of bento-box content layouts. Choose one for the content zone inside the frame. Cells sit on the surface token; at most one cell is .focal (accent fill). Grid = columns x rows with optional cell spans.

presets.grid: top-left focal (2x2) + L of 4 wrapping it

rules: cap at 4-6 cells for legibility (4x2 / 3x3 dashboards are the dense exceptions)
at most ONE focal cell per layout

motion.$source: Measured from ASML (7 slides / 153 build chunks) + INFO-3110 (48 slides / 184 build chunks). BUS McCormick_Presentation_C.pptx is static PowerPoint (no animations) and is a content reference only.
motion.$encoding: Keynote stores every animation as an animationAttributes record {animationType, effect, duration, delay, isAutomatic, delivery}. animationType = In | Out | Action | Transition. Two effect namespaces: apple:* (cross-iWork) and com.apple.iWork.Keynote.* (Keynote-only). Text builds decompose into buildChunks by delivery unit.
signature: Entrance-only: every build is type 'In'. No Build Out, no Action builds.
Dissolve is the spine, and TEXT RESOLVES PER CHARACTER, not as a block.
Motion lives WITHIN slides (builds), not between them: slide transitions are mostly 'none'.
Not a Magic Move / Morph user.

delivery.note: A trailing ' character' on an effect id binds that effect to per-character delivery.

fade_and_move_character.use: text enters per glyph with rise

movie_start.use: embedded video auto-plays as a build

transitions.measured: ASML 0/7 slides transition; INFO-3110 10/48 dissolve, rest cut.
